content: Administrative/instructor-initiated drops are always reason-coded (non-attendance, prerequisite not met, disciplinary, course cancelled, etc.) and formally logged via the registrar process rather than a silent removal. The student is expected to be able to see why they were dropped — reason codes exist precisely so the student and registrar have a documented, auditable cause. This is treated as baseline/table-stakes behavior in every SIS reviewed, not an edge case.
